summary: Inbox omnichannel end-to-end review. Backend pytest: 25/26 passing (1 failure + 0 flaky). Frontend: Bandeja UI fully functional — list, filters (channel/status/unread), search, open conv, composer, contact panel, SSE 'En vivo', close/reopen, mobile layout with back button, and regression across Hoy/Pipeline/Contactos/Agentes IA/Integraciones — all green. ONE BACKEND BUG FOUND: POST /api/inbox/conversations/{id}/send on an Instagram conv returns HTTP 409 'Canal instagram no conectado' instead of the spec'd 200 with send_result.ok=false + persisted outbound message status='failed'. Root cause in /app/backend/inbox.py: `except HTTPException: raise` re-raises composio_send's 409 before `upsert_conversation_and_message` runs, so the failed-outbound bubble never appears in the UI. SSE /events works (comment 'connected' observed <1s; invalid token → 401). Webhook dev-mode flow validated: created inbox conv, unread incremented, convo appears with unread_only=true filter. ENVIRONMENT restored to production.

critical.issue: Returns 409 'Canal instagram no conectado' instead of 200 with send_result.ok=false + persisted message.status='failed'. Root cause: `except HTTPException: raise` (inbox.py ~line 394) re-raises before calling upsert_conversation_and_message. Fix: catch HTTPException explicitly and convert to send_result={'ok':False,'error':exc.detail,'status_code':exc.status_code} so the outbound message still persists with status='failed' and the UI shows the red 'Falló' bubble.

ui_bugs.issue: When backend 409s, UI shows native alert 'Canal instagram no conectado' but NO message bubble with red 'Falló' tint is added (spec requires it). This is a downstream effect of the backend bug: since backend returns 409 instead of 200 with persisted failed message, there is no failed bubble to render. Will auto-resolve once backend is fixed.

action_items: Fix /api/inbox/conversations/{id}/send to catch HTTPException from composio_send and persist a failed outbound message instead of bubbling 409. Expected response: 200 + send_result.ok=false + message.status='failed'. This also unblocks the red 'Falló' bubble UI requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: inbox.py line ~394: `except HTTPException: raise` short-circuits the persist path — the whole design goal of 'user sees the message marked failed' is lost for any HTTPException (409 no conectado, 503 no api key, etc.). Swap to: except HTTPException as exc: send_result = {'ok': False, 'error': exc.detail, 'status_code': exc.status_code}.

inbox.py `_get_db()` creates a new Motor client per request — for a hot endpoint like /summary and /conversations this adds connection overhead. Consider a module-level singleton or reuse server.py's `db` dependency.

inbox.py list_conversations does in-memory text search AFTER the Mongo query+limit — works for small N but breaks semantics with pagination (search over visible page only). Acceptable MVP; document or switch to $text index before scaling.

realtime.py in-memory pub/sub is single-process only. With multiple gunicorn workers, SSE clients on worker A will miss publish() from worker B. Fine for now (single uvicorn worker) but needs Redis pub/sub before horizontal scale.
inbox.py backfill_conversations runs on every startup and walks the entire crm_messages collection — once indexes grow, add a `backfill_done` sentinel in a meta collection to short-circuit.
Good: cross-workspace isolation enforced via {id, workspace_id} lookups, 404 returned correctly when querying other tenant's conv id. Verified with pasta-admin hitting a digiactiva conv.
Good: /api/inbox/events uses ?token= (EventSource can't set headers) — correct pattern; validates token type='access' and workspace access.

success_rate.backend: 96% (25/26 pytest; the 1 failure is the documented send bug)
success_rate.frontend: 95% on verified flows (all UI OK; red Falló bubble blocked by backend bug)

test_credentials: founder@digiactiva.com / digiactiva2025 (founder_full); admin@pastaalvuelo.com / pastaalvuelo2025 (premium). Login returns JSON key 'token' and user.active_workspace_id.
seed_data_creation: Webhook dev test injected 1 Instagram contact per run with sender_id 'inbox_e2e_test_<ts>' in DigiActiva workspace (creates crm_conversations + crm_messages rows). Patch test temporarily set a conv status=closed+tags=[vip,test] then reset to open+[]. ENVIRONMENT toggled development→production in /app/backend/.env around webhook test (already restored to production).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All /api/inbox/* endpoints are live. data-testids in use: inbox-section, inbox-sse-status, inbox-list, inbox-search, inbox-filter-{all|whatsapp|instagram|messenger}, inbox-status-{open|pending|closed|all}, inbox-filter-unread, inbox-conv-{id}, inbox-conversation-pane, inbox-messages, inbox-composer, inbox-send-btn, inbox-close-btn, inbox-reopen-btn, inbox-back, inbox-toggle-contact, inbox-contact-panel, inbox-refresh. After fix to inbox.py send handler, re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_inbox.py::TestSend -v — expected 2/2 pass (web_chat 409 + instagram 200 with failed message). Then frontend re-check: open an instagram conv, type + send, confirm a grey/red 'Falló' bubble appears in [data-testid='inbox-messages'].
rca of the issue: BUG: /api/inbox/conversations/{id}/send returns 409 for instagram instead of 200+persisted-failed. REPRO: login as founder, GET /api/inbox/conversations?channel=instagram, take first id, POST /send with {message:'x'} → 409 'Canal instagram no conectado'. ROOT CAUSE: inbox.py send_message() wraps composio_send in try/except that re-raises HTTPException (line ~394-395). composio_send raises 409 when COMPOSIO_API_KEY is empty, bypassing the persist-regardless branch. FIX (main agent): replace `except HTTPException: raise` with `except HTTPException as exc: send_result = {'ok': False, 'error': exc.detail, 'status_code': exc.status_code}` so upsert_conversation_and_message+$set status='failed' runs and returns 200.
